3-4 Years - Pre-Maths & Science.title: EARLY CHILDHOOD EDUCATION SYLLABUS  —  3-4 YEARS  —  PRE-MATHEMATICS AND SCIENCE

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 0.1.1.1.1 Grouping things according to their characteristics
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Sorting Living things and non-living things in the environment
• Counting living things and non-living things in the environment 1-5
• Exploring places where living things are found
• Matching familiar livings things according to the places they are found.
• Creating patterns using natural materials from the local environment
• Measuring lengths of different things
• Comparing lengths of different things
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Things grouped according to their attributes.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Counting orally numbers 1 - 10
• Counting living and nonliving things 1-5
• Identifying numbers 1- 5 using living and non-livings
• Identifying written numbers (1 – 5) using living and non-livings
• Naming numbers (1- 5) using living and non-livings
• Counting numbers up to 10 using variety of living and non-livings
• Moulding numbers 1 – 5 using different materials
• Tracing numbers 1 – 5 using different materials
• Adding living and non-living things up to the sum of 5.
• Subtracting living and non-living things up to the difference of 4.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Numbers 1-10 recognised correctly

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying of coins and small notes up to K5

• Making a choice of what to buy and other means (batter system).
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Money up to K5 recognised correctly

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:

• Participating in waste management activities such as Reduce, Reuse, Recycle…
• Comparing lengths and widths of different objects
• Measuring length and width of different objects using nonstandard units.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Waste managed accordingly

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 0.2.1.1.1. Grouping things according to their characteristic

Animals (e.g. cats, Insects, chicken, cow, dog, snake…) and non-living things (e.g. Stones, Soil,Water Toys, crayons, pencil, book…...) according to given criteria such as quantity and size.

• Identifying living things: Plants (e.g. trees, Grass…) Animals: (e.g cat chicken, Insects, cow, dog, snake, Worms…..) and non-living things (e.g. Water, stone, Soils) toys, crayons, pencil, book).
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Things grouped according to their characteristics

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:

• Comparing lengths and widths of different objects
• Measuring length and width of different things using nonstandard units.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Waste managed accordingly

• Using pictures, diagrams, and models of the human body to identify the body parts associated with the five senses and their functions
• Demonstrating care for the five senses
• Matching the body parts associated with the five senses with the five senses.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • The parts of the human body associated with senses cared for accordingly

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 0.2.3.1.1 Tell time by activities of the day

• Recognising the seasons (cold, hot and wet) in Zambia through games, stories songs…
• Observing weather changes,
• Comparing pictures of the same place during different seasons,
• Discussing why some changes happen over time.
• Recognising effects of climate change in the local environment
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Tell time of the day correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:

cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Formation of shadows demonstrated correctly.

Grade 1 - English Language.title: GRADE 1 LOWER PRIMARY SYLLABUS  —  ENGLISH LANGUAGE

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 1.1.1.1 Use appropriate greetings at different times of the day
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Demonstrating greetings at different times of the day (good morning in the morning, good afternoon in the afternoon, good evening in the evening …)
• Responding to greetings at different times of the day (good morning – good morning, good afternoon – good afternoon, good evening – good evening …)
• Using and responding to common greetings (how are you? I am fine/I am not fine. How is home? Home is fine)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate greetings at different times of the day used correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 1.1.2.1 Use appropriate language to talk about members of a nuclear family
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Mentioning members of a nuclear family (father, mother, child/children)
• Talking about relationships that exist within the nuclear family (parent/parents’ brother, sister…)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to talk about members of a nuclear family used accordingly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Responding to simple commands (Follow the leader: "clap your hands”, “sit down”, “jump up and down”)
• Roleplaying in groups giving and responding to simple commands (Point to the door, get your pencil, touch your shoulders…)
• Playing games on giving and responding to commands (Simon says …touch your ears)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Simple commands given and followed accordingly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 1.1.4.1 Use appropriate language to talk about classroom rules
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Talking about classroom rules (use good language, keep the classroom clean, be friendly, ask for permission – “May I…”)
• Roleplaying scenarios following classroom rules.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to talk about classroom rules used correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 1.1.5.1 Use appropriate language to discuss chores and responsibilities of family members
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Naming chores of family members (table-setting, drying dishes, washing utensils, picking leaves…)
• Naming responsibilities of family members (caring, obedience)
• Discussing responsibilities of family members (parents providing for the family, children respecting and obeying parents, everyone taking care of each other when sick or in need, sharing food…)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to discuss chores and responsibilities of family members used accordingly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 1.1.6.1 Use appropriate language to tell time of the day, days of the week and months of the year
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Telling time of the day (morning, afternoon, night)
• Mentioning and sequencing days of the week (Sunday, Monday, Tuesday…)
• Mentioning and sequencing months of the year (January to December)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to tell the times of the day, days of the week and months of the year used correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 1.1.7.1 Use appropriate language to talk about different foods and meals of the day
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying different types of foods (fruits, vegetables, grains…)
• Mentioning types of meals (breakfast, lunch & supper/ dinner)
• Reciting a prayer before meals
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to talk about different foods and meals used appropriately.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 1.1.8.1 Use appropriate language to talk about occupations in the community
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Naming occupations in the community (teacher, nurse, police officer, shop keeper, drivers, watchman, farmer, …)
• Stating the role played by people in different occupations in the community (“teacher – to teach, police officer – to protect, nurse – caring for sick people…”)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to talk about occupations in the community used accordingly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 1.1.9.1 Use appropriate language to talk about things found at home
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying things found at home (kitchen – plates, spoons...; sitting room - TV, sofas, tables…; bedroom - bed, blankets…)
• Stating the use of things found at home (Plate - for eating, Sofa - for sitting on…).
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to talk about things found at home used accordingly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 1.1.10.1 Use appropriate language to talk about things found at school
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Naming things found at school (desks, chairs, books, chalkboard, flag, pencils, erasers, playground, ruler…)
• Stating the use of things at school (Chair – for sitting, Pencil – for writing…)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to talk about things found at school used accordingly.

cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to describe domestic animals used correctly.

cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language for buying and selling used accordingly.

• Demonstrating good practices in the community (picking litter, arranging the classroom …)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to talk about community practices used accordingly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 1.1.1.1 Distinguish groups of things according to their characteristics
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Exploring different things in the environment
• Collecting things from the environment according to the specified characteristics

• Describing groups (sets) using things
• Matching groups into one-to-one correspondence. Number of objects can be in intervals of 1 – 6, 1 – 9, 1 - 20 and up to 100
• Ordering groups according to their ordinal (1st, 2nd, 3rd …) and cardinal (1, 2, 3…) numbers.
• Assigning numerals 0 to 10 to elements in a set. Assign numerals in intervals of 0 – 6, 0 – 10.
• Using cardinal and ordinal numbers in everyday life
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Group of things distinguished according to their attributes

• Exploring activities involving zero to show that when zero is added to a number the result is that number
• Adding numbers using composition and decomposition of numbers (number bonds)
• Adding numbers using frames of 10
• Adding single digit numbers with a sum up to 18 (using the concept of ten as a unit)
• Adding whole numbers with sums up to 100 without carrying
• Completing addition of number sentences
• Using addition to carry out activities involving money and other real-life quantities
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Whole numbers used in addition in different situations correctly

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 1.5.2.2 Use whole numbers in subtraction things
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Subtracting single digit whole numbers in intervals of 1 to 6, 1 to 9 using concrete and semi concrete objects and transit to abstract by using the subtraction symbol as: i. take away ii. finding the difference iii. finding missing parts or finding less.
• Carrying out activities involving zero to show that: i. When zero is subtracted from a number the result is that number ii. When a number is subtracted from itself the result is zero
• Subtracting double digit numbers up to 18 using the unit of 10
• Subtracting whole numbers in 10s up to 100
• Subtracting whole numbers up to 100.
• Relating subtraction to addition such as 6 – 2 = 4 or 4 + 2= 6
• Completing subtraction of number sentences.
• Using subtraction to carry out activities involving money and other real-life quantities
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Subtraction of whole numbers used in different situations correctly

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:

Grade 3 - English Language.title: GRADE 3 LOWER PRIMARY SYLLABUS  —  ENGLISH LANGUAGE

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.1.1.1 Use appropriate language to discuss seasons of the year
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Describing the activities and actions associated with the seasons of the year (Rainy Season-carrying an umbrella, planting… Cold Season-wearing warm clothes… Dry Season-harvesting…)
• Discussing causes (pollution, deforestation…) and effects (drought, flooding…) of climate change on the seasons.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to discuss seasons of the year used accordingly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.1.2.1 Use appropriate language to discuss places in the community
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying groups of people found in different places in the community (School – pupils, teachers. Hospital – doctors, nurses, patients. Church – elders, pastors, members, priest…)
• Describing activities done in various places of the community (places of worship – praying, hospitals – treatment and care, police station – security)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to discuss places in the community used correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.1.3.1 Use appropriate language to discuss community responsibilities
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Mentioning community responsibilities (caring for public property, participation in community work, charity work…)
• Discussing the importance of community responsibilities (promotes community health and safety...)
• Participating in various community responsibilities (cleaning the environment, planting a garden…)
• Discussing the completed above activity using language.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to discuss community responsibilities used correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.1.4.1 Use appropriate language in asking and giving simple directions
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Discussing vocabulary for asking and giving simple directions (forward, backward, left, right, turn, opposite, next to, North, South, East, West…)
• Asking for directions (‘Excuse me, how do I get to the police station?’ ‘Where can I find a hospital? Thank-you)
• Giving directions (Walk down the road then turn left/right.)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language in asking and giving simple directions used correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.1.5.1 Use appropriate language in giving and following simple instructions
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Discussing vocabulary for giving and following instructions (first, second, add, stir, next, then, get, put…)
• Role playing giving and following instructions e.g how to make a paper boat (First fold a paper in half, next fold-in each corner….)
• Discussing the importance of community responsibilities (promotes community health and safety...)
• Participating in various community responsibilities (cleaning the environment, planting a garden…)
• Discussing the completed above activity using language.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language in giving and following simple instructions used correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.1.4.1 Use appropriate language in asking and giving simple directions
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Discussing vocabulary for asking and giving simple directions (forward, backward, left, right, turn, opposite, next to, North, South, East, West…)
• Asking for directions (‘Excuse me, how do I get to the police station?’ ‘Where can I find a hospital? Thank-you)
• Giving directions (Walk down the road then turn left/right.)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language in asking and giving simple directions used correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.1.5.1 Use appropriate language in giving and following simple instructions
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Discussing vocabulary for giving and following instructions (first, second, add, stir, next, then, get, put…)
• Role playing giving and following instructions e.g how to make a paper boat (First fold a paper in half, next fold-in each corner….)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language in giving and following simple instructions used correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.2.1.1 Use appropriate language to demonstrate manners and good behaviour
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Talking about communication etiquette (listening before speaking, do not speak or interrupt someone who is speaking…), good behaviour (respect for culture, honesty, empathy…) and appropriate dressing (cleanliness, modesty…)
• Making polite requests (magic word “please”, “May I” …)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Language to demonstrate manners and good behaviour used appropriately.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.3.1.1 Analyse simple stories and texts orally
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Listening to simple stories and texts on different themes (gender issues, human rights, health and nutrition…)
• Re-telling simple stories and texts (sequencing events in a story or text)
• Answering oral questions based on the story (main character(s), setting, plot, and important events in a story)
• Answering oral questions based on the text - expository (gender issues, human rights, health and nutrition…)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Simple stories and texts analysed correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Listening to and singing simple songs (Phonics songs, National Anthem, Road Safety Songs…)
• Answering oral questions based on the song (theme, rhyme scheme, characterisation)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Simple songs analysed correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.5.1.1 Use appropriate language to participate in simple plays
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Dramatising simple plays (voice projection, stage movement, public speaking, confidence
• Answering oral questions based on the play (theme, plot, setting, characterisation)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to participate in simple plays used correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES: • Reciting simple poems and rhymes in groups and individually.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Simple poems recited accurately.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Reciting riddles (What gets wetter as it dries? A towel. If you speak its name you break it. Silence)
• Interpret simple riddles (What has a tail and a head but no body? A coin…)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Riddles interpreted correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.8.1.1 Use appropriate language to describe people and objects
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying descriptive words (intelligent, clean, large, brave…)
• Describing people using appropriate adjectives (smart, kind, slow, helpful, polite, mean, selfish, greedy…)
• Describing objects using appropriate adjectives (striped, round, floral, spotted, sharp…)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to describe people and objects used accordingly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.8.2.1 Use appropriate language to describe events
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES: • Identifying descriptive words (exciting, boring, exhausting…)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Appropriate language to describe events used accordingly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.9.1.1 Sound alternative spelling choices of consonants
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying the alternative consonant spelling choices j – dge (bridge) s (treasure) w - u (penguin)
• Sounding out the consonant alternative spelling choices
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Alternative spelling choices of consonants sounded correctly.

cells.SUB-TOPIC: 3.9.2 Blend and read words using alternative consonants
cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.9.2.1 Blend and read words using alternative consonants
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Blending words with alternative consonant spelling choices (bridge, treasure, measure…)
• Reading words and sentences that contain alternative consonant spelling choices (pleasure, fridge. There is treasure under the bridge.)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Words with alternative consonants blended and read correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.9.3.1 Sound alternative spelling choices of vowels
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying the alternative vowel spelling choices /u/ - o (glove) /oo/ - ew, ue (glue, screw) Schwa – (teacher)
• Sounding out the alternative vowel spelling choices
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Alternative spelling choices of vowels sounded correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.9.3.2 Blend and read words with alternative spelling choices of vowels
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Blending words with alternative vowel spelling choices (love, crew, blue, circus…)
• Reading words and sentences that contain words with alternative vowel spelling choices (chew, collar, doctor. My teacher has blue gloves.)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Words with alternative spelling choices of vowels blended and read correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.9.4.1 Sound alternative spelling choices of digraphs

• Sounding out the alternative digraph spelling choices
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Alternative spelling choices of digraphs sounded correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.9.4.2 Blend and read words with alternative spelling choices of digraphs
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Blending words with alternative digraph spelling choices (position, station, chef, machine…)
• Reading words and sentences that contain alternative digraph spelling choices (action, machine. We found the chef at the station.)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Alternative spelling choices of digraphs used correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.9.5.1 Sound alternative spelling choices of trigraphs
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying alternative trigraph spelling choices /ear/ eer (beer) /air/ are, ear (bare, bear) /oor/ ure, (pure)
• Sounding out alternative trigraph spelling choices
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Alternative spelling choices of trigraphs sounded correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.9.5.2 Blend and read words with alternative trigraph spelling choices
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Blending words with alternative trigraph spelling choices (peer, cheer, fare, pear, bear, pure, cure…)
• Sounding out alternative trigraph spelling choices
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Words with alternative trigraph spelling choices blended and read correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.9.5.2 Blend and read words with alternative trigraph spelling choices

• Blending words with alternative trigraph spelling choices (peer, cheer, fare, pear, bear, pure, cure…)
• Reading words and sentences with alternative trigraph spelling choices (care, I was sure of the bus fare.)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Words with alternative trigraph spelling choices blended and read correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:

• Reading words with three-letter consonant blends (stream, spring, throne, shrine, screw, splash)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Words with consonant blends read correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.10.1.1 Read sight words in sentences with fluency
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Recognising high frequency sight words (every, goes, does, made, their, once, upon, always, also, eight, love, cover, after, want…)
• Reading high frequency sight words
• Reading high frequency sight words in sentences Subject + verb (SV I love you) Subject + verb + object (SVO John kicked the ball)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Sight words in sentences read fluently.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Discussing new vocabulary within the context of the story or text.

• Reading aloud more complex words, longer sentences and simple books with fluency.
• Asking questions, making predictions, and discussing key concepts.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Texts read aloud with fluency.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.12.1.1 Read simple stories and tex tswith understanding.
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Reading aloud in peer-reading sessions – different text types (fiction and non-fiction)
• Re-telling stories or texts to enhance comprehension.
• Asking and responding to comprehension questions based on main elements (story – characters, setting, plot…) central ideas and relevant details (informational text – theme, main points…)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Simple stories and texts read correctly with understanding.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.13.1.1R ead stories and texts with understanding

• Reading of fiction and non-fiction genres independently/ according to personal preference.

cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Stories and texts read correctly with understanding.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.14.1.1 Read a variety of stories and texts for pleasur e

• Reporting back new vocabulary from texts read.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • A variety of stories and texts read for pleasure accordingly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.15.1.1 Read a variety of stories and texts with understanding
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Reading a variety of stories and texts silently.

• Answering the 5 W+H question words (what, who, where, when, why, and how …)

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.16.1.1 Write legibly letters of the alphabet using cursive handwriting
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Writing in cursive lowercase letters - ascenders (b, d, l, f, h, t, k), descenders (g, j, p, q, y) and square letters (a, c, e, i, m, n, o, r, s, u, v, w, x)
• Copying words and sentences using cursive handwriting.
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Letters of the alphabet written legibly in cursive handwriting.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Playing the ‘clap and spell’ game to spell words (b-e-c-a-u-s-e)
• Building and writing words with the same spelling pattern – word families (ough – rough, tough. ake - cake, rake, bake…)
• Re-arranging letters to spell a selected word (mpuj = jump, thero = other)
• Creating a spelling journal (learners create a journal where they write words, sentences or short paragraphs)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Words spelt correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.17.2.1 Write dictated words, phrases and sentences
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Writing dictated words (hammer, square…)
• Writing dictated phrases (twenty empty boxes, an honest girl…)
• Writing dictated sentences (There are some happy children here…)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Dictated words, phrases and sentences written correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Matching words with similar meanings (clean – tidy, thin - skinny)
• Mapping given words with their synonyms (expensive – costly, dear, pricey, high…).
• Completing sentences using suitable synonyms (Annie is a beautiful girl (ugly, pretty)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Knowledge of synonyms demonstrated correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Matching words with opposite meanings (quiet – loud, gentle - rough)
• Mapping given words with their antonyms (sad – happy, excited, joyful, cheerful…)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Knowledge of antonyms demonstrated correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES: • Constructing sentences using: Subject + Verb (Patrick slept.) Subject + Verb + Object (Lufasi ate mangoes.) Subject + Verb + Direct Object + Indirect Object (Mutwale bought a cake for his mother.)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Simple sentences constructed correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying countable nouns (balls, pens) and uncountable nouns (salt, sugar, water)
• Classifying nouns into countable and uncountable (pencil, sand, grass, phone, hair, onion) Countable Uncountable Pencil Sand Phone Grass Onion Hair
• Construct sentences using countable and uncountable nouns.
• Adding 'es' and ‘ies’ to countable nouns to make the plural (bus + es = buses, baby + ies = babies)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Knowledge of nouns demonstrated correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Changing the form of the verb by using different pronouns (I jump, you jump, he/she/it jumps we jump, they jump)
• Completing sentences using the verb ‘to be’ (am, are, is) (I am singing, You are singing, He/she/it is singing, We are singing, They are singing)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Knowledge of verbs demonstrated correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying present simple tense (to walk, to cook), the present continuous tense (walking, cooking) and the past simple tense (walked, cooked)
• Changing regular verbs into the present continuous tense by adding the suffix ‘ing’ (cook + ing = cooking)
• Changing regular verbs into past tense by adding the suffix ‘ed’ (cook + ed = cooked)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Knowledge of tenses demonstrated correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Describing nouns using adjectives (black dog – old lady, fluffy coat, vicious animal, beautiful girl)
• Writing short sentences to describe nouns using adjectives (The red bag is mine…)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Knowledge of adjectives demonstrated correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.20.5.1 Demonstrate knowledge of adverbs of manner
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Describing verbs using adverbs of manner (walk slowly, eat hungrily, sing loudly)
• Filling in blank spaces with appropriate adverbs of manner (He/ She walked ……slow, slowly …..)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Knowledge of adverbs demonstrated correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying reflexive pronouns singular (I/myself, you/yourself, him/himself, her/herself, it/itself) and reflexive pronouns plural (we/ourselves, you/yourselves, them/themselves)
• Identifying reflexive pronouns in a text.
• Filling the blank spaces with appropriate reflexive pronouns (She felt very sorry for …………. = herself)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Knowledge of reflexive pronouns demonstrated correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.21.1.1 Punctuate sentences and short paragraphs with exclamation marks and speech marks
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Discussing the function of exclamation and speech marks.
• Identifying exclamation and speech marks in a text.
• Punctuating sentences and paragraphs using exclamation marks (!)
• Punctuating sentences in a short paragraph using speech marks (“….”)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Sentences and short paragraphs punctuated with exclamation and speech marks correctly.

cells.SPECIFIC COMPETENCES: 3.22.1.1 Sequence words and sentences in a logical order
cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Arranging words in alphabetical order (sun, stove, ship, sink = ship, sink, stove, sun)
• Re-arranging words in a sentence in logical order (girl Mary a is = Mary is a girl)
• Rearranging given sentences in logical order (She washed her face. Mwansa woke up in the morning. Mwansa then went to the market.) (Mwansa woke up in the morning. She washed her face. Mwansa then went to the market.)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Words and sentences sequenced in a logical order correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Scanning through a dictionary to find specific words
• Extracting definitions of specific words from a dictionary
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Dictionary used correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Identifying important ideas in a paragraph
• Writing important ideas in short form (phrases)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Simple paragraphs summarised correctly.

cells.LEARNING ACTIVITIES:
• Writing stories from picture strips (a dog fetching a ball; a child collecting eggs from a chicken, someone buying onions at the market)
• Composing stories using given vocabulary (eating, flying, laughing, clever, big, fast)
cells.EXPECTED STANDARD: • Stories written using given vocabulary correctly.
